elements.sent-2411-001: This paper presents a novel training matrix design for spatial modulation (SM) systems, by introducing a new class of two-dimensional (2D) arrays called sparse zero correlation zone (SZCZ) arrays.
elements.sent-2411-002: An SZCZ array is characterized by a majority of zero entries and exhibits the zero periodic auto- and cross-correlation zone properties across any two rows.
elements.sent-2411-003: With these unique properties, we show that SZCZ arrays can be effectively used as training matrices for SM systems.
elements.sent-2411-004: Additionally, direct constructions of SZCZ arrays with large ZCZ widths and controllable sparsity levels based on 2D restricted generalized Boolean functions (RGBFs) are proposed.
elements.sent-2411-005: Compared with existing training schemes, the proposed SZCZ-based training matrices have larger ZCZ widths, thereby offering greater tolerance for delay spread in multipath channels.
elements.sent-2411-006: Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed SZCZ-based training design exhibits superior channel estimation performance over frequency-selective fading channels compared to existing alternatives.
elements.sent-2411-007: Zero correlation zone (ZCZ) sequence sets were initially introduced in \cite{Fan99 .}
elements.sent-2411-008: These sequences are characterized by their zero periodic auto- and cross-correlation functions for certain time-shifts around the in-phase position.
elements.sent-2411-009: Throughout the years, numerous research attempts have been made on ZCZ sequence design \cite{Tang10,Torii04, Zhou08,Liu14,Super_182,Pai_23 .}
elements.sent-2411-010: From the application standpoint, ZCZ sequences can be employed as training sequences for optimal channel estimation performances in multi-input and multi-output (MIMO) systems \cite{Yuan_05,Yang_02,Hu_17 , spreading sequences in the quasi-synchronous code-division multiple-access (QS-CDMA) system \cite{Long98 , as well as pilot sequences for frequency synchronization and interference avoidance in multi-cell systems \cite{Zhang_10,Zhang_12 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: On the other hand, in recent years, spatial modulation (SM) has emerged as a promising multiple-antenna technique due to its unique feature that only one transmit antenna (TA) is activated per time-slot \cite{SM_2,SM_3,SM_4,SM_6,SM_5 .}
elements.sent-2411-012: Thanks to this feature, SM enjoys a number of advantages, such as zero inter-antenna interference over flat-fading channels, reduced complexity, and enhanced energy efficiency.
elements.sent-2411-013: Since only one TA is activated per time slot, channel estimation in SM systems requires sparse training matrices with each column containing only one non-zero entry.
elements.sent-2411-014: This contrasts with the traditional dense matrices used in conventional MIMO systems.
elements.sent-2411-015: A number of studies on channel estimation for SM systems over flat-fading channels have been conducted \cite{SM_Flat_2,SM_Flat_3,SM_timevaring_4,SM_timevaring_5 .}
elements.sent-2411-016: For SM channel estimation over frequency-selective channels, Liu {\it et al. introduced the cross Z-complementary pairs (CZCPs) \cite{CZCP-1st each having 1) front-end and tail-end ZCZs for their aperiodic autocorrelation sums and 2) tail-end ZCZ for their aperiodic cross-correlation sums.}}
elements.sent-2411-017: Due to these sequence pair properties, it is demonstrated that inter-symbol interference (ISI) and inter-antenna interference (IAI) can be mitigated when the delay spread is smaller than the relevant ZCZ widths of the CZCPs.
elements.sent-2411-018: Since then, various constructions of CZCPs have been developed \cite{Fan_20, Adhikary_20, Huang_20, Yang_21, Zeng_22, Das_22_ISIT, Zhang_22 .}
elements.sent-2411-019: As a further generalization, the CZCP concept has also been extended to the cross Z-complementary set (CZCS) \cite{Huang_22,Huang_22_ISIT .}
elements.sent-2411-020: However, the existing state-of-the-art primarily follows the training framework proposed by \cite{CZCP-1st , where several kernel CZCPs (or CZCSs) are arranged in a specific pattern to generate SM-compatible regular sparse matrices.}
elements.sent-2411-021: Consequently, the ZCZ widths of these training matrices are limited by the kernel CZCPs or CZCSs.
elements.sent-2411-022: Motivated by the aforementioned background, this paper presents a novel training framework for SM systems by introducing a new class of two-dimensional (2D) arrays called the sparse ZCZ (SZCZ) array.
elements.sent-2411-023: Our goal is to go beyond the existing kernel based approaches so as to offer a greater flexibility in SM training design.
elements.sent-2411-024: Formally, each SZCZ array is dominated by zero elements, and any two rows of such an SZCZ array exhibit the zero periodic auto- and cross-correlation zone property.
elements.sent-2411-025: By fully exploiting the sparsity and correlation properties of SZCZ arrays, we show that these SZCZ arrays can be directly used as training matrices for SM systems, thus referred to as SZCZ training matrices.
elements.sent-2411-026: It is worth mentioning that the ternary ZCZ arrays \cite{Chang_67,Xu_03,Wu_06 may not be feasible for the SM training matrices as they cannot guarantee the requirement of having only one non-zero entry per column.}
elements.sent-2411-027: In this paper, we introduce the concept of 2D restricted generalized Boolean functions (2D RGBFs).
elements.sent-2411-028: By carefully restricting certain variables in 2D RGBFs, we demonstrate that the positions of non-zero entries in each column of the corresponding 2D arrays can be controlled, which is crucial since only one non-zero entry is allowed for every SM training matrix column.
elements.sent-2411-029: Following this idea, we present direct constructions of SZCZ training matrices characterized by large ZCZ widths and controllable sparsity levels, utilizing 2D RGBFs.
elements.sent-2411-030: Compared with existing CZCP-based and CZCS-based training matrices, the proposed SZCZ training matrices possess ZCZ widths that are twice as large, providing greater tolerance for delay spread over frequency-selective channels.
elements.sent-2411-031: The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed SZCZ-based training design outperforms the performance of existing alternatives in channel estimation over frequency-selective fading channels.
elements.sent-2411-032: The subsequent sections of this paper are organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-033: In Section \ref{sec:background , we introduce some notations and preliminaries, including the SM systems and the definitions of the SZCZ array.}
elements.sent-2411-034: In Section \ref{sec:Training , we propose a novel training framework for SM systems based on the SZCZ array and present the constructions of SZCZ arrays.}
elements.sent-2411-036: Lastly, we provide the conclusion in Section \ref{sec:conclusion .}

elements.sent-2411-140: In this paper, we have proposed a novel training matrix design for SM systems.
elements.sent-2411-141: First, we have introduced a new class of 2D arrays, i.e., the SZCZ array, which consists of several zero entries.
elements.sent-2411-142: Any two rows of an SZCZ array possess the zero periodic auto- and cross-correlation zone property.
elements.sent-2411-143: By fully leveraging the sparsity and correlation properties of SZCZ arrays, we have shown that certain SZCZ arrays, where each column has only one non-zero entry, can be used as training matrices for SM systems, referred to as SZCZ training matrices.
elements.sent-2411-144: Furthermore, we have introduced the concept of 2D RGBFs and proposed direct constructions of SZCZ training matrices with large ZCZ widths and controllable sparsity levels in {\it Theorem \ref{thm:SZCZ_1 and {\it Theorem \ref{thm:SZCZ .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-145: Note that CZCP-based training matrices can be regarded as the special case of the proposed training matrices as stated in {\it Corollary \ref{crly:SZCZ_CZCP .}}
elements.sent-2411-146: Compared with existing CZCP-based and CZCS-based training matrices, the proposed SZCZ matrices exhibit larger ZCZ widths, indicating greater tolerance for delay spread, and do not require any CZCPs or CZCSs as kernel sequences.
elements.sent-2411-147: The simulation results indicate that the proposed SZCZ-based training design outperforms the performance of existing alternatives in channel estimation over frequency-selective fading channels.. \begin{appendices.}
